# AI Review Procedure

The AI should apply the playbook in a structured way. It should not provide final legal approval. It should identify issues, explain why the issue matters, propose fallback wording if available, and state whether escalation is required.


### 10.1 Mandatory AI rules

- Do not invent missing deal facts. If deal value, data scope, delivery model, client location, or governing law context is missing, mark the item as Missing Context.
- Separate internal legal notes from external comments to the counterparty.
- Do not disclose Trinetix escalation thresholds, risk appetite, or internal approval rules in external comments.
- Use Green / Yellow / Red / Missing Context ratings.
- Always check placeholders, cross-references, defined terms, party names, dates, notices, and signature blocks.
- Always check whether the SOW terms align with the MSA.
- For customer paper, map clauses to this playbook before proposing comments.
- For Trinetix paper, focus on deviations, missing project facts, placeholders, and business-specific completion.
